message.content:
Review the US-031 "Role-based router (creator vs lister tabs)" implementation for the Marketify Expo RN + expo-router codebase.

## Scope of review

New files:
- app/(auth)/_layout.tsx — bounces authed users; renders Slot
- app/(auth)/index.tsx — DEV-only sign-in buttons for creator/lister (placeholder role picker until US-032)
- app/(creator)/_layout.tsx — custom TabBar (workaround for Tabs crash) with Discover/Applied/Submitted/Profile
- app/(creator)/discover.tsx, applied.tsx, submitted.tsx, profile.tsx — placeholder tab screens
- app/(lister)/_layout.tsx — custom TabBar with Home/Campaigns/Inbox/Profile
- app/(lister)/index.tsx, campaigns.tsx, inbox.tsx, profile.tsx — placeholder tab screens; index hosts FAB
- src/components/shared/Fab.tsx — 56x56 cta FAB with press-collapse animation (reuses Button pattern)
- src/components/shared/TabBar.tsx — custom tab bar (renders over Slot); used by both creator and lister groups
- src/components/shared/TabPlaceholder.tsx — shared placeholder surface for tab screens

Modified:
- app/index.tsx — removed __DEV__ /primitives redirect, now routes by role via useAuth()

## Acceptance criteria (US-031)

- app/_layout.tsx wraps with AuthProvider + ToastProvider + GestureHandlerRootView (already done in prior stories — do not re-verify)
- Unauthed users see (auth) group
- Creators see (creator) tab group with Discover/Applied/Submitted/Profile
- Listers see (lister) tab group with Home/Campaigns/Inbox/Profile + FAB hook per docs/design.md §3.2
- Tab labels + lucide icons per docs/design.md §3.1 / §3.2
- One role per account (dual-role out of scope per scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md)

## Context you need

- **Codebase Patterns** worth checking: scripts/ralph/progress.txt top section. Notably:
  - Pattern #14: expo-router's native <Tabs> crashes under Expo Go + SDK 54 new-arch ("expected dynamic type 'boolean'"). This is why TabBar.tsx exists — we render <Slot /> with a custom tab bar underneath.
  - Pattern #29: route groups (parens) are invisible in URLs. TabBar splits `path` (for router.navigate, includes group) from `activePathname` (URL-stripped, for usePathname comparison).
  - Pattern #27: no MMKV v4 in Expo Go new-arch — use expo-secure-store (already in place for US-030).
- useAuth() is from src/lib/auth.tsx (US-030). Exposes `role: 'creator' | 'lister' | null`, `signIn(token, user)`, `signOut()`.
- docs/design.md §3.1 creator tabs = Discover / Applied / Submitted / Profile; §3.2 lister tabs = Home / Campaigns / Inbox / Profile + FAB (cta orange, 56pt, hard shadow).
- Neubrutalist design system in src/design/tokens.ts (colors, shadows.hard offset 3,3) and typography.ts (Rubik_700Bold for tab labels).
- Toast primitive has a pre-existing regression unrelated to US-031 (FAB.onPress calls show(...) but toast doesn't render — same issue on /(dev)/primitives toast-test buttons). Flag if you see a possible root cause in the reviewed files, otherwise ignore.

## What I want from you

Flag issues rated medium or higher. I'll fix them before committing. Focus on:
- Correctness: auth redirect loops, broken routing, stale state
- Type safety: any missing types, implicit any, Href typing
- Design-spec conformance: icons, labels, colors, tab order
- Accessibility: testID, accessibilityLabel, roles
- Code smells specific to RN/expo-router (effect cleanup, Pressable gotchas, SafeArea placement)
- Whether the DEV-only sign-in in (auth)/index.tsx is adequately gated by __DEV__

Do NOT re-litigate the <Tabs>-vs-custom-TabBar decision; that's been settled by Pattern #14. Do NOT suggest improvements unrelated to US-031's scope.

Report findings with file:line refs and a brief rationale. Under 300 words total if possible.

content.text: I'll read the relevant files to review the US-031 implementation.

1	import { SafeAreaView, StyleSheet, Text, View } from 'react-native';
2	import { ButtonPrimary, ButtonSecondary } from '@/components/primitives/Button';
3	import { colors, spacing } from '@/design/tokens';
4	import { textStyles } from '@/design/typography';
5	import { useAuth, type AuthUser } from '@/lib/auth';
6	
7	// Placeholder role picker — US-032 builds the two large pressables per
8	// docs/design.md §2.1. For US-031 this screen exists so the (auth) group has
9	// something to render; the __DEV__ buttons below let testers flip into a tab
10	// group without the real auth-* edge functions.
11	export default function AuthIndex() {
12	  const { signIn } = useAuth();
13	
14	  return (
15	    <SafeAreaView style={styles.container}>
16	      <View style={styles.content}>
17	        <Text style={[textStyles.display, styles.title]}>Marketify</Text>
18	        <Text style={[textStyles.body, styles.subtitle]}>
19	          Pick your role to continue. The full picker lands in US-032.
20	        </Text>
21	
22	        {__DEV__ ? (
23	          <View style={styles.devBlock}>
24	            <Text style={[textStyles.caption, styles.devCaption]}>DEV ONLY</Text>
25	            <ButtonPrimary
26	              label="Sign in as creator"
27	              onPress={() => signIn(DEV_CREATOR_TOKEN, DEV_CREATOR_USER)}
28	              accessibilityLabel="Dev sign in as creator"
29	              testID="dev-signin-creator"
30	            />
31	            <View style={styles.spacer} />
32	            <ButtonSecondary
33	              label="Sign in as lister"
34	              onPress={() => signIn(DEV_LISTER_TOKEN, DEV_LISTER_USER)}
35	              accessibilityLabel="Dev sign in as lister"
36	              testID="dev-signin-lister"
37	            />
38	          </View>
39	        ) : null}
40	      </View>
41	    </SafeAreaView>
42	  );
43	}
44	
45	const DEV_CREATOR_TOKEN = 'dev-creator-token';
46	const DEV_LISTER_TOKEN = 'dev-lister-token';
47	
48	const DEV_CREATOR_USER: AuthUser = {
49	  id: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001',
50	  username: 'dev_creator',
51	  email: null,
52	  role: 'creator',
53	  created_at: new Date(0).toISOString(),
54	  updated_at: new Date(0).toISOString(),
55	  deleted_at: null,
56	};
57	
58	const DEV_LISTER_USER: AuthUser = {
59	  id: '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002',
60	  username: 'dev_lister',
61	  email: 'dev-lister@example.com',
62	  role: 'lister',
63	  created_at: new Date(0).toISOString(),
64	  updated_at: new Date(0).toISOString(),
65	  deleted_at: null,
66	};
67	
68	const styles = StyleSheet.create({
69	  container: {
70	    flex: 1,
71	    backgroundColor: colors.canvas,
72	  },
73	  content: {
74	    flex: 1,
75	    alignItems: 'stretch',
76	    justifyContent: 'center',
77	    paddingHorizontal: spacing.xl,
78	    gap: spacing.base,
79	  },
80	  title: {
81	    color: colors.ink,
82	    textAlign: 'center',
83	  },
84	  subtitle: {
85	    color: colors.ink70,
86	    textAlign: 'center',
87	    marginBottom: spacing.xxl,
88	  },
89	  devBlock: {
90	    gap: spacing.sm,
91	  },
92	  devCaption: {
93	    color: colors.ink40,
94	    textAlign: 'center',
95	    letterSpacing: 1,
96	  },
97	  spacer: {
98	    height: spacing.md,
99	  },
100	});
101

1	import { Redirect, Slot } from 'expo-router';
2	import { Compass, Send, User, Video } from 'lucide-react-native';
3	import { StyleSheet, View } from 'react-native';
4	import { TabBar, type TabDef } from '@/components/shared/TabBar';
5	import { colors } from '@/design/tokens';
6	import { useAuth } from '@/lib/auth';
7	
8	// Creator tab group — 4 tabs per US-031 AC + docs/design.md §3.1
9	// (Discover / Applied / Submitted / Profile). We render `<Slot />` with a
10	// custom `<TabBar />` rather than expo-router's native `<Tabs>` because the
11	// latter crashes under Expo Go + SDK 54 new-arch (Codebase Pattern: Stack +
12	// Tabs throw "expected dynamic type 'boolean'"). A dev-client build would let
13	// us swap back to `<Tabs>` without changing any screens.
14	const CREATOR_TABS: TabDef[] = [
15	  {
16	    label: 'Discover',
17	    icon: Compass,
18	    path: '/(creator)/discover',
19	    activePathname: '/discover',
20	    testID: 'tab-discover',
21	  },
22	  {
23	    label: 'Applied',
24	    icon: Send,
25	    path: '/(creator)/applied',
26	    activePathname: '/applied',
27	    testID: 'tab-applied',
28	  },
29	  {
30	    label: 'Submitted',
31	    icon: Video,
32	    path: '/(creator)/submitted',
33	    activePathname: '/submitted',
34	    testID: 'tab-submitted',
35	  },
36	  {
37	    label: 'Profile',
38	    icon: User,
39	    path: '/(creator)/profile',
40	    activePathname: '/profile',
41	    testID: 'tab-profile',
42	  },
43	];
44	
45	export default function CreatorLayout() {
46	  const { role } = useAuth();
47	  if (role !== 'creator') return <Redirect href="/(auth)" />;
48	
49	  return (
50	    <View style={styles.root}>
51	      <View style={styles.content}>
52	        <Slot />
53	      </View>
54	      <TabBar tabs={CREATOR_TABS} />
55	    </View>
56	  );
57	}
58	
59	const styles = StyleSheet.create({
60	  root: { flex: 1, backgroundColor: colors.canvas },
61	  content: { flex: 1 },
62	});
63

1	import { Redirect, Slot } from 'expo-router';
2	import { Building2, Inbox, LayoutDashboard, Megaphone } from 'lucide-react-native';
3	import { StyleSheet, View } from 'react-native';
4	import { TabBar, type TabDef } from '@/components/shared/TabBar';
5	import { colors } from '@/design/tokens';
6	import { useAuth } from '@/lib/auth';
7	
8	// Lister tab group — Home (index) / Campaigns / Inbox / Profile per US-031 AC
9	// + docs/design.md §3.2. Same custom-TabBar rationale as the creator layout:
10	// expo-router's native `<Tabs>` crashes in Expo Go + SDK 54 new-arch. The FAB
11	// is mounted on the Home screen per §3.2 ("Create-campaign is a FAB on
12	// Dashboard + Campaigns tabs — not a center-tab bump").
13	const LISTER_TABS: TabDef[] = [
14	  {
15	    label: 'Home',
16	    icon: LayoutDashboard,
17	    path: '/(lister)',
18	    activePathname: '/',
19	    testID: 'tab-home',
20	  },
21	  {
22	    label: 'Campaigns',
23	    icon: Megaphone,
24	    path: '/(lister)/campaigns',
25	    activePathname: '/campaigns',
26	    testID: 'tab-campaigns',
27	  },
28	  {
29	    label: 'Inbox',
30	    icon: Inbox,
31	    path: '/(lister)/inbox',
32	    activePathname: '/inbox',
33	    testID: 'tab-inbox',
34	  },
35	  {
36	    label: 'Profile',
37	    icon: Building2,
38	    path: '/(lister)/profile',
39	    activePathname: '/profile',
40	    testID: 'tab-profile',
41	  },
42	];
43	
44	export default function ListerLayout() {
45	  const { role } = useAuth();
46	  if (role !== 'lister') return <Redirect href="/(auth)" />;
47	
48	  return (
49	    <View style={styles.root}>
50	      <View style={styles.content}>
51	        <Slot />
52	      </View>
53	      <TabBar tabs={LISTER_TABS} />
54	    </View>
55	  );
56	}
57	
58	const styles = StyleSheet.create({
59	  root: { flex: 1, backgroundColor: colors.canvas },
60	  content: { flex: 1 },
61	});

1	# Marketify — UI/UX Design Document
2	
3	*Mobile app for the creator marketing industry. Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase.*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Design Language
8	
9	### 1.1 Chosen Style
10	
11	**Neubrutalism** as the primary language, with disciplined restraint for mobile comfort.
12	
13	**Why it fits Marketify:**
14	- The marketplace/directory pattern (Vibrant & Block-based) is the top match; Neubrutalism is its most purposeful expression — bold borders, hard offset shadows, flat pop colors.
15	- The pattern rewards scannability — hard black outlines on CampaignCards make bounties feel like physical "cards on a board."
16	- WCAG AAA by construction (high contrast), Excellent performance — critical for mid-tier Android.
17	- Differentiated. TikTok is dark-glassmorphic, Instagram is quiet gradient, every campaign-management tool (Grin, Aspire) is corporate-blue SaaS. Neubrutalism carves out a clear creator-native identity.
18	
19	**Disciplines to keep it mobile-friendly (not a desktop stunt):**
20	- Borders are 2px, not 4px.
21	- Shadows are 3px offset max, never 6–8px.
22	- Corner radius is 12px on cards, 999px on pills — not 0. Pure 0 feels brittle on touch.
23	- No intentionally "broken" layouts. Brutalism's anti-design aesthetic is skipped in favor of its craft elements only.
24	
25	### 1.2 Color Palette
26	
27	Base palette anchored on the **"Creator Economy Platform"** colors (pink + orange CTA), with semantic statuses added.
28	
29	| Role | Token | Hex | Usage |
30	|---|---|---|---|
31	| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
32	| Primary Soft | `--primary-soft` | `#FDF2F8` | Primary-tinted backgrounds, selected chip bg |
33	| Primary Deep | `--primary-deep` | `#831843` | Primary text on soft bg, pressed states |
34	| Accent (CTA) | `--cta` | `#F97316` | All primary action buttons, "Apply", "Submit", "Create Campaign" |
35	| Accent Deep | `--cta-deep` | `#9A3412` | CTA pressed state |
36	| Ink (text) | `--ink` | `#0F172A` | All body and heading text, hard borders |
37	| Ink-70 | `--ink-70` | `#475569` | Secondary text, captions |
38	| Ink-40 | `--ink-40` | `#94A3B8` | Tertiary / disabled text |
39	| Surface | `--surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card bodies |
40	| Canvas | `--canvas` | `#FFF9F2` | App background (warm off-white — matches orange CTA undertone) |
41	| Hairline | `--hairline` | `#E5E7EB` | Dividers inside cards |
42	| **Success (approved)** | `--success` | `#16A34A` | Approved submissions, approved applications, eligible checks |
43	| Success Soft | `--success-soft` | `#DCFCE7` | Approved pill bg |
44	| **Danger (rejected)** | `--danger` | `#DC2626` | Rejected submissions, rejected applications |
45	| Danger Soft | `--danger-soft` | `#FEE2E2` | Rejected pill bg, ineligibility warning |
46	| **Warning (pending)** | `--warning` | `#F59E0B` | Pending status, waiting-for-review states |
47	| Warning Soft | `--warning-soft` | `#FEF3C7` | Pending pill bg |
48	| **Cancelled** | `--cancelled` | `#6B7280` | Auto-cancelled applications |
49	| Cancelled Soft | `--cancelled-soft` | `#F3F4F6` | Cancelled pill bg |
50	| Shadow | `--shadow` | `#0F172A` | Neubrutalist hard shadow (always ink, never tinted) |
51	
52	**Contrast targets (measured against `--canvas` `#FFF9F2`):**
53	- Body text (`--ink` `#0F172A`): **17.8:1** — passes WCAG AAA.
54	- Secondary text (`--ink-70` `#475569`): **7.9:1** — passes AAA.
55	- Primary brand pink (`--primary` `#EC4899`) on canvas: **~3.3:1** — passes WCAG AA for large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold) and UI components, but **does not clear AAA**. Primary pink is therefore **never used for body text** — only for brand marks, large display accents, active-tab icons (paired with a text label), and soft-tinted backgrounds. Where pink needs to carry meaning on light backgrounds at body size, we use `--primary-deep` `#831843` (contrast ~11:1, AAA).
56	- CTA orange (`--cta` `#F97316`) is a background color under black label text (17.8:1), so the button itself is AAA; the orange is never used as foreground text.
57	
58	**Dark mode:** deferred for MVP. Neubrutalism translates cleanly (invert canvas → `#0B0B0F`, keep shadow as a white 1px border with a `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` offset), but it doubles visual QA.
59	
60	### 1.3 Font Pairing
61	
62	**"Startup Bold"** — `Clash Display` for headings, `Satoshi` for body. Both via `expo-font` (Fontshare CDN or self-host). Fallbacks: `Outfit` for headings, `Rubik` for body.
63	
64	| Role | Font | Weight | Size (mobile) |
65	|---|---|---|---|
66	| Display (screen titles, campaign titles) | Clash Display | 600–700 | 28 / 22 |
67	| Heading | Clash Display | 600 | 20 |
68	| Subhead | Satoshi | 600 | 17 |
69	| Body | Satoshi | 500 | 15 |
70	| Caption | Satoshi | 500 | 13 |
71	| Micro (pills, chip labels) | Satoshi | 700 | 11 (uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`) |
72	| Monospace (URLs, handles @xxx) | JetBrains Mono | 500 | 14 |
73	
74	**Usage rules:**
75	- Display font is *only* for titles and hero metric numbers. Never stack two Clash Display elements in the same card.
76	- User-generated content (campaign descriptions) bumps to 16px.
77	- Line-height: 1.55 body, 1.15 display.
78	- Line-length: max 65 characters → content width caps at ~340px on a 390px screen.
79	
80	**Dynamic Type / system font scale.** Honor iOS Dynamic Type and Android font scale up to **130%** on all text — all sizes above scale proportionally via `allowFontScaling={true}` (RN default). **Cap at 130%** to protect Neubrutalism's fixed line-heights (1.15 display) and the 340px content-width cap, both of which break visually past that threshold (letters start touching; cards overflow gutters). Above 130% we clamp the scale factor in a top-level `Text.defaultProps.maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3` setting — the OS-level preference is still reflected up to the cap, just not beyond it. For users who need more, the v2 accessibility settings screen will expose a "Full size-up (may break layout)" escape hatch. Pills, status chips, and tab-bar labels use `allowFontScaling={false}` because their containers are fixed — they're UI chrome, not content.
81	
82	### 1.4 Motion Language (Reanimated)
83	
84	**Spring presets** (define in `animations/springs.ts`):
85	```
86	soft:    { damping: 20, stiffness: 180, mass: 1 }   // cards, chips
87	snappy:  { damping: 18, stiffness: 260, mass: 1 }   // tab switch, pill transitions
88	bouncy:  { damping: 12, stiffness: 220, mass: 1 }   // status change celebration
89	```
90	
91	**Timing presets:**
92	```
93	micro:  150ms  ease-out     // press feedback
94	short:  220ms  ease-out     // enter/exit
95	medium: 320ms  cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)  // screen transitions
96	```
97	
98	**Motion rules:**
99	1. **Screen transitions** — stack push uses native `slide_from_right` (expo-router default). Modals use `modal` presentation with a spring slide-up (snappy).
100	2. **Press feedback** — every pressable scales to 0.97 on `pressIn` (spring.soft), returns on `pressOut`. Use `Pressable` + `useAnimatedStyle` with `useSharedValue`. Never use `TouchableOpacity`.

31:| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
55:- Primary brand pink (`--primary` `#EC4899`) on canvas: **~3.3:1** — passes WCAG AA for large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold) and UI components, but **does not clear AAA**. Primary pink is therefore **never used for body text** — only for brand marks, large display accents, active-tab icons (paired with a text label), and soft-tinted backgrounds. Where pink needs to carry meaning on light backgrounds at body size, we use `--primary-deep` `#831843` (contrast ~11:1, AAA).
105:7. **Tab bar** — active tab icon morphs from `strokeWidth=1.5` outline to `strokeWidth=2.5` + filled enclosed areas via a paired ship-in-repo SVG (crossfaded, 180ms). Active indicator is a 4px bar under the icon (spring.snappy).
120:| Iconography | **Lucide React Native** exclusively. 24×24 viewBox, 2px stroke. Tab bar uses **stroke-weight toggle**, not filled variants — Lucide doesn't ship filled glyphs for every icon we need (`video`, `megaphone`, `inbox`). Inactive tab: `strokeWidth={1.5}`; active tab: `strokeWidth={2.5}` + fills the glyph's enclosed areas (e.g. compass needle) via a paired `fill` SVG we ship ourselves for the 4 creator + 4 lister tab icons. No emoji. |
316:- **"Continue in background"** at any time → immediate route to the feed; outstanding runs complete in the background and update Profile metrics silently. A small `MetricStaleIndicator state='refreshing'` chip appears in the Profile tab's icon dot until all runs land.

729:| **EmptyState** | Generic empty | `illustration: EmptyStateIllustrationKey, title, body, primaryAction, secondaryAction?` — `EmptyStateIllustrationKey` is a string-keyed registry: `'no_campaigns' \| 'no_eligible' \| 'no_applications' \| 'no_submissions' \| 'lister_no_campaigns' \| 'lister_no_applications' \| 'lister_no_submissions' \| 'network_error' \| 'not_found'`. Each key maps to a ship-in-repo SVG (`assets/illustrations/<key>.svg`) using only `--primary` / `--cta` / `--ink` per §1.5 |
737:| **TabBar** | Custom tab bar per role | `tabs[], activeIndex` |
738:| **NotificationRow** | Inbox item | `type, title, timestamp, read, href` |
740:| **OverrideEligibilityDialog** | Approve-time confirmation for listers overriding a failed post-condition; renders the failed-conditions summary + required typed confirmation | `failedConditions[], onConfirm, onCancel` |
753:- 5-tab bars.
769:- [ ] All content text respects system Dynamic Type / font scale up to 130% (`maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3`); UI chrome (pills, tab labels, status chips) opts out with `allowFontScaling={false}`

[Showing results with pagination = offset: 100]

31:| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
55:- Primary brand pink (`--primary` `#EC4899`) on canvas: **~3.3:1** — passes WCAG AA for large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold) and UI components, but **does not clear AAA**. Primary pink is therefore **never used for body text** — only for brand marks, large display accents, active-tab icons (paired with a text label), and soft-tinted backgrounds. Where pink needs to carry meaning on light backgrounds at body size, we use `--primary-deep` `#831843` (contrast ~11:1, AAA).
105:7. **Tab bar** — active tab icon morphs from `strokeWidth=1.5` outline to `strokeWidth=2.5` + filled enclosed areas via a paired ship-in-repo SVG (crossfaded, 180ms). Active indicator is a 4px bar under the icon (spring.snappy).
120:| Iconography | **Lucide React Native** exclusively. 24×24 viewBox, 2px stroke. Tab bar uses **stroke-weight toggle**, not filled variants — Lucide doesn't ship filled glyphs for every icon we need (`video`, `megaphone`, `inbox`). Inactive tab: `strokeWidth={1.5}`; active tab: `strokeWidth={2.5}` + fills the glyph's enclosed areas (e.g. compass needle) via a paired `fill` SVG we ship ourselves for the 4 creator + 4 lister tab icons. No emoji. |
164:| Dashboard | `/(lister)/dashboard` | Stat tiles + recent activity | "Create campaign" (FAB) |
166:| My Campaigns | `/(lister)/campaigns` | List active + inactive | Tap card |
169:| Applications Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` | Review applicants with metrics | Tap row |
171:| Submissions Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions` | Review final videos | Tap row |
196:| Inbox | Trigger | Banner copy |
198:| Lister Applications Inbox | any pending application `created_at > 7 days` | *"N applications pending > 7 days. Review or close the listing."* |
199:| Lister Submissions Inbox | any pending submission `created_at > 5 days` | *"N submissions waiting > 5 days. Creators are watching the clock."* |
206:## 3. Navigation Structure
208:### 3.1 Tab Bar — Creator (4 tabs)
212:| Feed | compass | Discover | `/(creator)/feed` |
213:| Applications | send | Applied | `/(creator)/applications` |
214:| Submissions | video | Submitted | `/(creator)/submissions` |
219:### 3.2 Tab Bar — Lister (4 tabs)
224:| Campaigns | megaphone | Campaigns | `/(lister)/campaigns` |
225:| Inbox | inbox | Inbox | `/(lister)/inbox` (apps + submissions, segmented) |
228:Create-campaign is a **FAB** (cta color, hard shadow, 56px) on Dashboard + Campaigns tabs — not a center-tab bump.
230:**Inbox model — relationship to §2.3 IA.** §2.3 lists Applications Inbox and Submissions Inbox as *campaign-scoped* routes (`/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions`) because review always happens in-context for a specific campaign. The **Inbox tab** (`/(lister)/inbox`) is a cross-campaign roll-up — same underlying data, different entry point:
232:- **Segmented header:** `Applications · Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs — keeps the 4-tab bar).
237:So the campaign-scoped routes are the sources of truth; the Inbox tab is a lens. This matches how lister work actually happens — sometimes you think "that Nike campaign has applicants" (campaign-first), sometimes "do I have anything to review?" (inbox-first).
239:### 3.3 Modal vs Stack vs Tab
253:### 3.4 Deep Link Strategy
259:| Creator app approved | `marketify://applications/:id` | Application Detail with animated status change | Applied tab → Application Detail |
260:| Creator app rejected | `marketify://applications/:id` | Application Detail, rejection reason expanded | Applied tab → Application Detail |
261:| Creator app cancelled (listing edited) | `marketify://applications/:id?reason=listing_edited` | Application Detail with "What changed" diff card | Applied tab → Application Detail |
262:| Submission approved/rejected | `marketify://submissions/:id` | Submission Detail with status change | Submitted tab → Submission Detail |
263:| Lister new application | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/applications/:aid` | Application Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Applications Inbox → Application Review |
264:| Lister new submission | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/submissions/:sid` | Submission Review | Home tab → Campaign Detail (`:cid`) → Submissions Inbox → Submission Review |
267:Built with `expo-router`. **Back-stack seeding rule:** deep links hydrate a realistic navigation spine, not just the target screen on top of the home tab. The table above spells out each link's spine — back-swipe from an Application Review lands the lister on the Applications Inbox they would have reached manually, not on Dashboard. One-level-deep links (creator Application Detail, Profile) seed a single parent tab and stop there.
349:│ │ Discover                  🔔│ ← 28pt Clash Display, bell right-aligned
685:### 5.5 Empty State — No Eligible Campaigns
689:- **Body:** "Campaigns get added daily. Meanwhile, here's what to try."
724:| **FAB** | Floating action (Lister) | `icon, onPress` |
737:| **TabBar** | Custom tab bar per role | `tabs[], activeIndex` |
738:| **NotificationRow** | Inbox item | `type, title, timestamp, read, href` |
753:- 5-tab bars.
769:- [ ] All content text respects system Dynamic Type / font scale up to 130% (`maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3`); UI chrome (pills, tab labels, status chips) opts out with `allowFontScaling={false}`

content.text: Now I have enough information to produce a precise review. Let me check one more thing about the lister home route discrepancy and the FAB positioning.

content.content:
205	
206	## 3. Navigation Structure
207	
208	### 3.1 Tab Bar — Creator (4 tabs)
209	
210	| Tab | Icon | Label | Stack root |
211	|---|---|---|---|
212	| Feed | compass | Discover | `/(creator)/feed` |
213	| Applications | send | Applied | `/(creator)/applications` |
214	| Submissions | video | Submitted | `/(creator)/submissions` |
215	| Profile | user | Profile | `/(creator)/profile` |
216	
217	Notifications are a bell button in every tab's header (lower traffic, avoids 5-tab trap).
218	
219	### 3.2 Tab Bar — Lister (4 tabs)
220	
221	| Tab | Icon | Label | Stack root |
222	|---|---|---|---|
223	| Dashboard | layout-dashboard | Home | `/(lister)/dashboard` |
224	| Campaigns | megaphone | Campaigns | `/(lister)/campaigns` |
225	| Inbox | inbox | Inbox | `/(lister)/inbox` (apps + submissions, segmented) |
226	| Profile | building-2 | Profile | `/(lister)/profile` |
227	
228	Create-campaign is a **FAB** (cta color, hard shadow, 56px) on Dashboard + Campaigns tabs — not a center-tab bump.
229	
230	**Inbox model — relationship to §2.3 IA.** §2.3 lists Applications Inbox and Submissions Inbox as *campaign-scoped* routes (`/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions`) because review always happens in-context for a specific campaign. The **Inbox tab** (`/(lister)/inbox`) is a cross-campaign roll-up — same underlying data, different entry point:
231	
232	- **Segmented header:** `Applications · Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs — keeps the 4-tab bar).
233	- **Default segment:** whichever has the higher unreviewed count; ties go to Submissions (higher creator-anxiety cost per day of delay).
234	- **Row grouping:** grouped by campaign with `SectionHeader` rows; tapping a row pushes into the campaign-scoped review screen (same route as §2.3).
235	- **Empty state:** if both segments empty, "Nothing to review. Good time to post another bounty." with a CTA button to Create Campaign.
236	
237	So the campaign-scoped routes are the sources of truth; the Inbox tab is a lens. This matches how lister work actually happens — sometimes you think "that Nike campaign has applicants" (campaign-first), sometimes "do I have anything to review?" (inbox-first).
238	
239	### 3.3 Modal vs Stack vs Tab

content.text:
Here is the review.

---

## Review: US-031 Role-based Router

### Critical

**1. Creator tab routes diverge from design spec (confidence: 95)**

`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/_layout.tsx` lines 18–42

`docs/design.md §3.1` specifies the stack roots as `/(creator)/feed`, `/(creator)/applications`, and `/(creator)/submissions`. The implemented routes are `/discover`, `/applied`, and `/submitted` — different route segments. When US-038/039/040 implement those screens they will either be placed at the wrong path or require a retroactive rename that breaks any existing deep links. The correct `path` values should be `/(creator)/feed`, `/(creator)/applications`, `/(creator)/submissions`, and the corresponding files should be named `feed.tsx`, `applications.tsx`, `submissions.tsx`.

**2. FAB is absent from the Campaigns tab (confidence: 90)**

`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns.tsx`

`docs/design.md §3.2` explicitly states: "Create-campaign is a FAB on **Dashboard + Campaigns tabs**." The FAB is present on the Home (index) screen but entirely absent from `campaigns.tsx`. This is a spec omission, not a future-story concern — the FAB chrome is already built.

---

### Important

**3. Lister Dashboard route mismatch (confidence: 85)**

`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/_layout.tsx` line 14 / `app/(lister)/index.tsx`

`docs/design.md §3.2` names the stack root `/(lister)/dashboard`. The implementation uses `/(lister)` (index route). The `activePathname` is `'/'` (line 21 of `_layout.tsx`), which means `isActive` short-circuits on the `tabActivePath === '/'` guard (TabBar.tsx line 64) and can never match a nested route under a future `/(lister)/dashboard/` subtree. If US-048 adds a child screen, the Home tab will stop lighting up. Aligning to `/dashboard` now avoids this.

**4. `accessibilityRole` on tab Pressables is `"button"` instead of `"tab"` (confidence: 85)**

`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/components/shared/TabBar.tsx` line 41

React Native's accessibility system recognises `"tab"` as a valid role. Using `"button"` means VoiceOver/TalkBack reads these as generic buttons with no indication they are navigation tabs. `accessibilityState={{ selected }}` is correctly set but only meaningful when the role is `"tab"`. Fix: `accessibilityRole="tab"`.

**5. DEV constants are module-level but not in a `__DEV__`-guarded block (confidence: 80)**

`/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(auth)/index.tsx` lines 45–66

`DEV_CREATOR_TOKEN`, `DEV_LISTER_TOKEN`, `DEV_CREATOR_USER`, and `DEV_LISTER_USER` are unconditionally present in the module even in production bundles. Metro's dead-code elimination for `__DEV__` is reliable for `if (__DEV__)` branches in JSX but does **not** tree-shake top-level `const` declarations. The fake token strings and well-known UUIDs will appear in a production bundle. Move all four constants inside the `if (__DEV__)` branch (or into a separate `dev-fixtures.ts` file guarded at import time) to ensure they are stripped.
